inline_comments.message: is there no way to determine this programmatically?

There is one endpoint https://<openstack IP>:5000, which gives supported keystone API versions. 
Example:
[root@localhost ~(keystone_admin)]# curl http://10.237.166.212:5000
{"versions": {"values": [{"status": "stable", "updated": "2018-02-28T00:00:00Z", "media-types": [{"base": "application/json", "type": "application/vnd.openstack.identity-v3+json"}], "id": "v3.10", "links": [{"href": "http://10.237.166.212:5000/v3/", "rel": "self"}]}, {"status": "deprecated", "updated": "2016-08-04T00:00:00Z", "media-types": [{"base": "application/json", "type": "application/vnd.openstack.identity-v2.0+json"}], "id": "v2.0", "links": [{"href": "http://10.237.166.212:5000/v2.0/", "rel": "self"}, {"href": "https://docs.openstack.org/", "type": "text/html", "rel": "describedby"}]}]}}[root@localhost ~(keystone_admin)]#

But there is no way to determine what user prefers.
Example: Openstack Ocata supports both v2.0 and v3.
If the keystone API version is configurable, then user can configure preferred keystone version.
Another issue with the above endpoint is, it says v2.0 status as deprecated even though Ocata supports it. Hence it is not reliable.
i think we should take the latest version that is available. We do this for all other apis
if its v2 then auth_token is null ?
